channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:
1. 93-year-old Gene Kranz shares how he felt watching the Artemis II mission  Upworthy
  2. Opinion | Generation X Goes to the Moon  WSJ
  3. 'A big deal': Western NC Apollo veteran reacts to Artemis II splashdown  WLOS
  4. Artemis II: When Gen Z was busy perfecting moon-tinted filters, Gen X was travelling to the far side of the Moon  The Times of India
  5. Sparking wonder across generations  Southern Utah News

item.title: Graphene as a charge mirror: Why water droplets 'see' graphene—but don't show it - Phys.org
item.description: Graphene as a charge mirror: Why water droplets 'see' graphene—but don't show it  Phys.org
